findings.evidence: The new public API exports `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_eq_zero_iff`, `_mul`, `_one`, `_prod`, and lift equivalences at lines 65, 98, 103, 107, 85/92, but `rg -n "elementaryTwoQuotientMk_.*eq|eq_iff" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean` finds no equality-of-representatives lemma.
findings.fix: Add and annotate a characteristic lemma such as `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_eq_iff` stating `elementaryTwoQuotientMk g = elementaryTwoQuotientMk h ↔ IsSquare (g / h)` (or the equivalent multiplicative relation), and mirror it in `TauCeti.ClassGroup`.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_eq_zero_iff` only characterizes when one representative is trivial; there is no public criterion for equality of two quotient classes, so users must unfold `ModN`/the quotient relation to prove `elementaryTwoQuotientMk g = elementaryTwoQuotientMk h`.

summary: The quotient surface is useful but its characteristic API is incomplete for downstream use without unfolding. Add the missing equality/representative lemmas for the class map.
